comment: FR-SPD-004 frame-blend slow motion: a 1/2x clip over 64 fps frame-index-coded media (green = frameIndex mod 256) sampled at timeline frame 511, which maps to source frame 255.5 - exactly between frame 255 (green 255) and frame 256 (green 0, the mod-256 wrap chosen so the two adjacent frames differ maximally). The correct blend mixes the two frames at weight 1/2 in LINEAR working light (ADR-0010) and re-encodes with the Rec.709 OETF, giving green byte ~180; a display-encoded blend would give ~128 and is rejected by the executor unit test. Wrong-variant calibration measured against this reference: dropping frameSampling (nearest, current single-frame decode) fails with maxDeltaE=41.4; blending with the wrong weight (sampling source frame 255.25, weight 1/4 instead of 1/2) fails with maxDeltaE=23.0. Tolerance 10 is ~2x the measured cross-machine ProRes/color-conversion noise floor (maxDeltaE 4.614 on the CI runner vs 0.000 locally) and the weakest wrong-variant signal (23.0) is >= 2x the tolerance, matching the FR-SPD-002 fixture calibration convention.
